intro: Rorippa microphylla, also known as small-leaved watercress or yellow watercress, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The plant is rich in nutrients and has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in protecting against cell damage and reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. The bioactive constituents of Rorippa microphylla include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support eye health and treat conditions such as conjunctivitis and cataracts.
